research_experience.title: Computational Biology Research Intern
bullets: Worked to assist Dr. Jordan Dowell’s biology research lab in analyzing image data related to fungi
Created an open-source, modular, and easy-to-use software package (SkelPy) with Python and PyQt that automates the creation of skeletons from images of fungal structures
Wrote extensive documentation on how to use and modify SkelPy for a researcher’s specific purpose
SkelPy on Github: https://github.com/AaronMoseley/SkelPy

research_experience.title: Medical Imaging/Machine Learning Research Assistant
bullets: Worked in Dr. Abdullah-Al-Zubaer Imran’s lab to find novel training techniques and architecture improvements for medical image segmentation models
Created an innovative contrastive learning strategy and multi-tasking architecture that was shown to significantly improve performance over baselines in segmenting the liver from CT scans
Awarded an Undergraduate Engineering Research Fellowship for Fall 2023 by the University of Kentucky College of Engineering

research_experience.title: Nuclear Physics Research Assistant
bullets: Worked with Dr. Christopher Crawford as part of the Neutron Optics Parity and Time Reversal Experiment (NOPTREX)
Used C++, ROOT framework, and HDF5Lib to create a high-speed data acquisition system for the NOPTREX experiment investigating gamma radiation emissions from decaying neutrons
Validated the data acquisition system in a successful test at Japan Proton Accelerator Research Complex

experience.title: Machine Learning Engineer
bullets: Leading development and release of multiple new full-length ML pipelines, including custom data annotation tools, model architectures, and post-processing steps, aimed at detecting the boundaries of materials, roadways, curbs, and paint lines in aerial images
Designed two novel model architectures with PyTorch leveraging semi-supervised and multi-task learning, both resulting in more than a 20% improvement over previous strategies
Developing extensive tooling for ground-truth data creation using C++, PyQt, and SQL to transform dozens of drone-captured orthomosaics into usable labeled data for computer vision models
Developed modular plugin system for a point cloud visualization software using C++ and ZeroMQ
Created HTTP server, API, and web pages to automate and visualize ML model training, inference, and evaluation

experience.title: Computer Engineering Intern
bullets: Developed physical verification rules in Siemens’ Standard Verification Rule Format (SVRF) and designed transistor-level validation cells for unit testing using Cadence Virtuoso
Created comprehensive Vim syntax highlighter for SVRF using Vimscript

experience.title: Engineering and Technology Intern
bullets: Built data analysis and report automation tools using VBA framework and Microsoft Excel
Completed projects creating $33,000 in annual savings and reducing time spent generating reports by 90%
Held secret-level United States security clearance with the U.S. Department of Defense

teaching_experience.title: Computer Graphics and UI (CS335) Grader
bullets: Graded assignments and exams for more than 60 students on topics covering UI development, computer graphics, and image manipulation
Regularly held office hours for students with questions on grading and assignments

teaching_experience.title: Intermediate C++ and Linux (CS216) Teaching Assistant
bullets: Regularly lectured multiple lab sections on topics including intermediate C++, Unix, Bash, and Vim
Provided individualized help for students during lab and outside of class time
Held weekly office hours and graded classwork and exams for more than 30 students

teaching_experience.title: Introductory C++ and Linux (CS215) Teaching Assistant
bullets: Provided lab instruction covering introductory C++ in conjunction with course instructor
Held office hours and graded coursework and exams for over 25 students

education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science and Mathematics
bullets: Member of the Lewis Honors College
Placed 1st in Kentucky at the 2024 International Collegiate Programming Contest Mid-Central Regionals
Earned the Department of Computer Science Award for Outstanding Academic Achievement
Placed on the Dean’s List for all semesters
Earned the Provost Scholarship and Lester Engineering Scholarship
Member of the Undergraduate Science Journal Club

projects.name: Context-aware Multitasking for Medical Image Segmentation
bullets: Novel multi-tasking framework that combines segmentation and classification for medical images
Shows significant improvement over baseline and pre-trained models in Dice score and Haussdorf distance

projects.name: Volt Engine
bullets: An in-progress open source rendering engine built in Vulkan with C++, HLSL, Qt, and GLM
Provides a scene-object-component system with per-instance data modifiable at runtime
Implements a simple text-rendering system and Qt widgets around the render surface
A small lighting demo with a first-person controller is included in the repository that implements the Blinn-Phong algorithm with multiple light sources of varying colors and intensities

projects.name: Shoulder-Mounted Space Program
bullets: A game built in Unity with C# where the player launches themselves from planet to planet with a rocket launcher as the only form of travel
Uses a custom realistic gravity simulation to determine the movement of planets, debris, and the player
Includes two unique game modes, 15 levels that each introduce a new mechanic, and a final boss fight
Shoulder-Mounted Space Program was released on Steam on December 17, 2025
